title: Yawal Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Yawal Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Maharashtra

overview.short: The Yawal Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in the Jalgaon district of Maharashtra, India, at the banks of the Anner and Manjar rivers and at the border of Madhya Prad
overview.description: The Yawal Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in the Jalgaon district of Maharashtra, India, at the banks of the Anner and Manjar rivers and at the border of Madhya Pradesh. The sanctuary covers an area of approximately 176 km2 (68 sq mi) and is characterized by its extensive dense forest coverage. The area was officially recognized as protected in 1969. Encroachment, deforestation and tree smuggling led to significant habitat degradation starting in the early 2000s.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Yawal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Yawal Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Yawal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jalgaon Airport (53 km) and the nearest railway station is Aurangabad Railway Station (177 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Yawal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Yawal Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Yawal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Yawal Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Yawal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Garbaldi Dam.
